data.title: URA launches two new residential sites for sale
data.description:
The tender for the Bedok Rise site will close at 12 noon on 27 November 2025.

The Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA) has launched two residential sites for sale today under the second half of the 2025 Government Land Sales (GLS) Programme.

The sites at Bedok Rise and Cross Street can potentially yield about 380 residential units and 315 long-stay serviced apartments, respectively.

The tender for the Bedok Rise site will close at 12 noon on 27 November 2025.

data.title: Transport ministry unveils strategy to boost national connectivity
data.description:
Amongst the strategies is increasing Changi Airport’s capacity with the launch of Terminal 5.

The Ministry of Transport has unveiled its strategy to better connect Singapore by air, land, and sea.

According to acting Minister for Transport Jeffrey Siow, the government expects to operate Changi Terminal 5 by mid-2030. This new facility will increase the airport’s annual passenger capacity by more than 50% to serve about 50 million more passengers, and grow city links from around 170 to over 200.

There is also the Changi East Industrial Zone and the improved Changi Airfreight Centre that will increase air cargo handling capacity by 80% to 5.4 million tonnes per annum.

For the Tuas Port, its expansion to be completed in the 2040s will expand its handling capacity by  60% to 65 million twenty-foot equivalent units annually. The consolidation of port facilities at Tuas will free up land in the city centre for urban development and green spaces.

Meanwhile, the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link will carry up to 10,000 passengers per hour, connecting Woodlands North to Bukit Chagar in five minutes. The line is expected to begin service by December 2026.

Under the Land Transport Master Plan, MOT plans to close the loop for the Circle Line, extend the Downtown Line and Thomson-East Coast Line, and progressively open the new Jurong Region Line. The Cross Island Line will link all existing lines when it is completed, providing an alternative route.

Bus connectivity will also be enhanced, with nearly $1b going into the Bus Connectivity Enhancement Programme to improve access and shorten public transport journey times for residents living in HDB estates further away from the city.

data.title: Boustead Singapore forms new industrial REIT
data.description:
The company intends to use the net proceeds of $57.8 million for potential acquisitions.

Boustead Singapore will form the UI Boustead REIT, which will invest in logistics, industrial, high-specification industrial and business space assets.

The REIT’s initial portfolio is expected to comprise 23 properties, with 21 leasehold properties in Singapore and two freehold properties located in Japan.

The IPO properties have a total gross floor area (GFA) of approximately 5.9 million square feet (sq ft) and a net lettable area of approximately 5.3 million sq ft. with a total property value of $1.9b.

The company intends to use the net proceeds of $57.8 million for potential acquisitions, working capital and other capital allocation decisions and reinvestment.

It will also acquire the remaining interest in four divestment properties, including 29 Media Circle, BP-BBD2, 84 Boon Keng Road, and 11 Seletar Aerospace Link, through various purchase agreements.

Boustead is expected to receive about $6.2m from the proposed divestments.

data.title: Centurion Accommodation REIT launches $771.1m IPO at $0.88 per unit
data.description:
Trading on the SGX Main Board will start on 25 September.

Centurion Accommodation REIT has launched an initial public offering (IPO) to raise about $771.1m through the sale of units at $0.88 each.

The IPO portfolio will consist of purpose-built worker and student accommodation across Singapore, the UK and Australia

Trading of the units on the Singapore Exchange’s Main Board is expected to begin at 2:00 pm on 25 September.

Projected distribution yields for the enlarged portfolio are 7.47% for 2026 and 8.11% for 2027.

The public offer opened at 10:00 pm on 18 September and will close at 12:00 noon on 23 September.

data.title: Lum Chang Holdings’ director disposes 1.02 million shares in company
data.description:
The shares were sold for a total of $433,451.

Lum Chang Holdings Limited’s managing director, David Lum Kok Seng, has disposed of 1,022,000 shares from Ellipsiz Limited, according to a bourse filing.

The shares were sold for a total of $433,451.16.

Lum’s total interest fell to 215.94 million shares from 216.96 million previously.

His stake in the company now stands at 57.64%

data.title: Boustead Singapore values proposed REIT at $1.9b
data.description:
It is expected to debut with a portfolio of 23 properties.

Boustead Singapore Limited has announced plans to launch UI Boustead REIT on the Singapore Exchange, with the IPO portfolio valued at $1.9b.

The REIT is expected to debut with a portfolio of 23 properties, comprising 21 in Singapore and two in Japan.

The assets have a gross floor area of about 5.9 million square feet and a net lettable area of 5.3 million square feet.

After the listing, Boustead and its subsidiaries are expected to hold up to 16.9% of the REIT’s units. The transactions will provide about $62.1m in cash proceeds.

As part of the preparation, Boustead signed agreements to divest stakes in four Singapore properties into the REIT.

data.title: Hatten Land seeks 180-day extension of judicial management order
data.description:
The company's shares have been suspended from trading since 6 August 2024.

Hatten Land Limited has applied to extend its judicial management order, which is set to expire on 9 October, for a further 180 days.

The company's shares have been suspended from trading since 6 August 2024.

If approved, the extension would keep the order in effect until 7 April 2026.

Meanwhile, the court has fixed the hearing for the extension application to be on 6 October at 10:00 am.

data.title: JTC grants Kaki Bukit Avenue 5 tender to Extra Space for $31.39m
data.description:
It was the sole bidder.

JTC has awarded the tender for the industrial site at Kaki Bukit Avenue 5 to the sole bidder Extra Space Kaki Bukit Pte. Ltd. at a tendered sum of $31.39m.

The site area is 6,903.5 sqm, with a tenure of 33 years. Project completion is expected to be in 60 months.

Launched on 27 May 2025, the tender closed on 22 July 2025.

data.title: Gov’t to review public housing income ceiling
data.description:
Singapore wants to provide more housing options for singles, seniors, and larger families.

The Ministry of National Development plans to review the public housing income ceiling and study how to provide more housing options, including for singles, seniors, and larger families

The ministry said it will keep public housing affordable and accessible through a robust supply of Build-To-Order flats across different regions, including more with shorter waiting times.

The government plans to improve public rental housing by upgrading older rental blocks and giving more support to rental households so that they can own their homes.

Meanwhile, the ministry also said it will step up upgrades in older towns and homes through schemes such as the Home Improvement Programme and Neighbourhood Renewal Programme, whilst rolling out more senior-friendly infrastructure.

Seven selected private housing estates will also receive enhancements under the Estate Upgrading Programme, and eligible households with elderly residents can get support to install in-home fittings under the Enhancement for Active Seniors (Private) scheme.

MND said it will expand integrated community hubs in areas like Toa Payoh and Woodlands, extend the parks and park connector network, and create new public spaces along “Identity Corridors”. It will also push climate-sensitive urban planning, building design and the use of cool paints to reduce the impact of rising temperatures.

data.title: Hongkong Land to sell MCL Land to Sunway for $739m
data.description:
The total capital recycled by the company since 2024 has reached $2.6b.

Hongkong Land Holdings Limited has signed an agreement to divest its Singaporean and Malaysian residential development business, MCL Land, to Sunway Group for its net asset value of $739m.

Following the transaction, Hongkong Land said the total capital it has recycled since 2024 has reached $2.6b (US$2b), representing 50% of the company’s target of at least $5.13b (US$4b) by the end of 2027.

The proceeds will be used to strengthen Hongkong Land’s balance sheet and contribute an additional $192.4m (US$150m) to the company’s ongoing share buyback programme.

Completion of the agreement is subject to standard closing conditions and is expected before year’s end.

US$1 = $1.28
